In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
from matplotlib.patches import Patch

sns.set_theme(rc={'figure.figsize': (16, 9)})
In [2]:
age_gander = pd.read_csv('Age-Gender.csv')
countries = pd.read_csv('Countries.csv')
placement = pd.read_csv('Placement.csv')

DF_age_gander

In [3]:
sorted_ads = sorted(
    age_gander['Ad-name'].dropna().unique(),
    key=lambda x: (x.split(' — ')[0], int(x.split(' — ')[1]))
)

for i in sorted_ads:
    print(i)

In [4]:
age_gander['Impressions'] = age_gander['Impressions'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [5]:
age_gander['Clicks'] = age_gander['Clicks'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [6]:
age_gander['Leads'] = age_gander['Leads'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [7]:
age_gander['CPL'] = age_gander['Spent'] / age_gander['Leads']
In [8]:
age_gander['CTR'] = age_gander['CTR'].str.replace('%', '').astype(float)
In [9]:
age_gander.info()
age_gander.head()

DF_countries

In [10]:
countries['Impressions'] = countries['Impressions'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [11]:
countries['Clicks'] = countries['Clicks'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [12]:
countries['CTR'] = countries['CTR'].str.replace('%', '').astype(float)
In [13]:
countries['Leads'] = countries['Leads'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [14]:
countries['CPL'] = countries['Spent'] / countries['Leads']

DF_placement

In [16]:
placement['Impressions'] = placement['Impressions'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [17]:
placement['Clicks'] = placement['Clicks'].str.replace(',', '').fillna(0).astype(int)
In [18]:
placement['CTR'] = placement['CTR'].str.replace('%', '').astype(float)
In [19]:
placement['Leads'] = placement['Leads'].fillna(0).astype(int)
In [20]:
placement['CPL'] = placement['Spent'] / placement['Leads']
In [21]:
placement.info()
placement.head()

Marketing_Analisis

Age & Gender

In [22]:
AGL = age_gander.groupby('Ad-name')['Leads'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
In [23]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(data=AGL[0:15], y='Leads', x='Ad-name')

for i in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(i)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()
In [24]:
AGA = age_gander.groupby('Age')['Leads'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
In [25]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(data=AGA[0:15], y='Leads', x='Age')

for i in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(i)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()
In [26]:
AGG = age_gander.groupby('Gender')['Leads'].sum().sort_values(ascending=False).reset_index()
In [27]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(data=AGG[0:15], y='Leads', x='Gender')

for i in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(i)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()

In [31]:
AG_TOP = (age_gander.groupby('Ad-name')['Leads'].sum().loc[lambda x: x >= 20].index)
In [32]:
CTRTOP =  (age_gander[age_gander['Ad-name'].isin(AG_TOP)].groupby(['Ad-name', 'Gender'], as_index=False)['CTR'].mean())
In [33]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

sns.barplot(data=CTRTOP[CTRTOP['Gender'] != 'unknown'],x='Ad-name',y='CTR',hue='Gender',errorbar=None,palette={'male': 'blue', 'female': 'red'})

plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right')
plt.title('CTR by Campaign and Gender (Top Campaigns by Leads)')
plt.show()

In [36]:
agg = AGL.groupby('Ad-name', as_index=False).agg({'Leads': 'sum','CPL': 'median'})

agg = agg.sort_values(by='CPL')

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 7))

sns.barplot(data=agg, x='Ad-name', y='Leads', ax=ax1, color='skyblue')
ax1.set_ylabel('Leads')

for container in ax1.containers:
    ax1.bar_label(container, fmt='%.0f', padding=3)

ax1.set_xticklabels(ax1.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha='right')

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
sns.lineplot(data=agg, x='Ad-name', y='CPL', ax=ax2, color='red', marker='o')
ax2.set_ylabel('CPL')

legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor='skyblue', label='Leads'),Line2D([0], [0], color='red', marker='o', label='CPL')]

ax1.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='upper left')
plt.title('Campaign Performance: Leads vs CPL')
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

/var/folders/cv/b1w8syyx55v6x9ttzq6gbh0c0000gn/T/ipykernel_2494/4248311061.py:13: UserWarning: set_ticklabels() should only be used with a fixed number of ticks, i.e. after set_ticks() or using a FixedLocator.
  ax1.set_xticklabels(ax1.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha='right')
In [37]:

In [38]:
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 6))

sns.heatmap(AGL.groupby(['Age', 'Ad-name'])['Leads'].sum().unstack(fill_value=0),
    annot=True,       
    fmt='.0f',        
    cmap='Blues'     
)

plt.title('Leads by Age and Campaign')
plt.xlabel('Ad-name')
plt.ylabel('Age')

plt.show()

In [47]:
ax = sns.barplot(data=CCTR_mean.sort_values(by='CTR', ascending=False)[0:30], x='Country', y='CTR', errorbar=None)
for i in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(i, fmt='%.2f')
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()
In [48]:
agg = CCTR.groupby('Country', as_index=False).agg({'Leads': 'sum','Spent': 'sum'})

agg = agg[agg['Leads'] > 0]
agg['CPL'] = agg['Spent'] / agg['Leads']

agg = agg.sort_values(by='CPL')

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 7))

sns.barplot(data=agg, x='Country', y='Leads', ax=ax1, color='skyblue')
ax1.set_ylabel('Leads')

for container in ax1.containers:
    ax1.bar_label(container, fmt='%.0f', padding=3)

ax1.set_xticklabels(ax1.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha='right')

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
sns.lineplot(data=agg, x='Country', y='CPL', ax=ax2, color='red', marker='o')
ax2.set_ylabel('CPL')

legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor='skyblue', label='Leads'),Line2D([0], [0], color='red', marker='o', label='CPL')]

ax1.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='upper left')
plt.title('Campaign Performance: Leads vs CPL')
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()

/var/folders/cv/b1w8syyx55v6x9ttzq6gbh0c0000gn/T/ipykernel_2494/2272198535.py:16: UserWarning: set_ticklabels() should only be used with a fixed number of ticks, i.e. after set_ticks() or using a FixedLocator.
  ax1.set_xticklabels(ax1.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha='right')
In [49]:
pivot = (CCTR.groupby(['Country', 'Ad-name'])['Leads'].sum().unstack())
pivot = pivot[pivot.sum(axis=1) > 0]


plt.figure(figsize=(14, 8))

sns.heatmap(
    pivot,
    annot=True,
    fmt='.0f',
    cmap='Blues',
    mask=(pivot == 0)
)

plt.title('Leads by Country and Campaign')
plt.xlabel('Ad-name')
plt.ylabel('Country')

plt.show()

Platform and Placement

In [87]:
PL = (placement[placement['Ad-name'].isin(C_TOP)][['Ad-name', 'Platform', 'Placement', 'Leads', 'CTR', 'Spent', 'CPL']].sort_values(by='Leads', ascending=False))
print(PL)

In [83]:
PL_sum = PL.groupby('Platform')['Leads'].sum().reset_index()
PL_sum = PL_sum[PL_sum ['Leads'] != 0]
print(PL_sum)

    Platform  Leads
1   Facebook   1695
2  Instagram    227
3  Messenger     23
In [84]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(data=PL_sum, y='Leads', x='Platform', errorbar = None)

for i in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(i)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()
In [85]:
PL_sum_placement = PL.groupby('Placement')['Leads'].sum().reset_index()
PL_sum_placement = PL_sum_placement[PL_sum_placement['Leads'] != 0]
In [86]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))

ax = sns.barplot(data=PL_sum_placement.sort_values(by='Leads', ascending=False), y='Leads', x='Placement', errorbar = None)

for i in ax.containers:
    ax.bar_label(i)

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.show()

In [88]:
agg = (PL.groupby('Placement', as_index=False).agg({'Leads': 'sum','Spent': 'sum'}))

agg = agg[agg['Leads'] > 0]
agg['CPL'] = agg['Spent'] / agg['Leads']
agg = agg.sort_values(by='CPL')

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 7))

sns.barplot(data=agg, x='Placement', y='Leads', ax=ax1, color='skyblue')
ax1.set_ylabel('Leads')

for container in ax1.containers:
    ax1.bar_label(container, fmt='%.0f', padding=3)

plt.setp(ax1.get_xticklabels(), rotation=45, ha='right')

ax2 = ax1.twinx()
sns.lineplot(data=agg, x='Placement', y='CPL', ax=ax2, color='red', marker='o')
ax2.set_ylabel('CPL')

legend_elements = [
    Patch(facecolor='skyblue', label='Leads'),
    Line2D([0], [0], color='red', marker='o', label='CPL')
]

ax1.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='upper left')
plt.title('Placement Performance: Leads vs CPL')
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [90]:
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))

sns.heatmap(PL.groupby(['Placement', 'Ad-name'])['Leads'].sum().unstack(fill_value=0),
    annot=True,       
    fmt='.0f',        
    cmap='Blues'     
)

plt.title('Leads by Placement and Campaign')
plt.xlabel('Ad-name')
plt.ylabel('Placement')

plt.show()
